AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 130 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ALONG FM 1277 JUST SOUTHWEST OF SAN AUGUSTINE, WHERE IT UPROOTED AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO WIDENED AND MOVED ACROSS CEDAR RD WHERE EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE LED TO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO 54 STRUCTURES IN A NEIGHBORHOOD. AN AWNING WAS RIPPED OFF OF A GAS STATION AND SNAPPED THE THE ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION TOWER OF A SUBSTATION NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 96 AND 21. A LAUNDROMAT AND SEVERAL ADDITIONAL STRUCTURES SUFFERED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE AS THE STORM MOVED ACROSS THE NORTHERN SIDE OF DOWNTOWN SAN AUGUSTINE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST WHILE UPROOTING AND SNAPPING NUMEROUS TREES ACROSS COUNTY ROAD 353 BEFORE LIFTING NEAR COUNTY ROAD 131 JUST SHY OF THE SHELBY COUNTY LINE. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 600 YARDS, WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 8.61 MILES.

THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED IN THE HOLMESVILLE COMMUNITY, WHERE IT DAMAGED A CHICKEN HOUSE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ROUTE 15 AND HOLMESVILLE RD. IT THEN CROSSED ROUTE 15 AND UPROOTED TREES ALONG ITS PATH TO LAKE D`ARBONNE. BEFORE IT CROSSED THE LAKE, IT UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES WHICH FELL ON A HOME THAT SUSTAINED MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE FROM THE FALLEN TREES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST SNAPPING AND UPROOTING MORE TREES, AND EVENTUALLY CROSSED ROUTE 143 (ARKANSAS RD.). IT LIFTED JUST SHORT OF THE UPPER OUACHITA NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE. PEAK WINDS EST. AT 110 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 21.45 MILES. MAX. WIDTH EST. AT 850 YARDS.
